hypothesis: Introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in ECL2/TM5 pockets while reducing off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are strong binders to SSTR2 but require further optimization to reduce off-target interactions. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are expected to increase selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we should focus on mutating non-pharmacophore positions that are likely involved in off-target interactions (SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts).
These mutations are designed to reduce off-target binding by altering residues that are likely to form conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining SSTR2 binding.
